title: Federal Reserve Bank of New York Policy on Counterparties for Market Operations

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York published its general policy framework governing relationships with private sector counterparties used for executing market operations. The policy outlines that the NY Fed transacts primarily with regulated banks and broker-dealers, sets credit risk and operational scale expectations, and requires counterparties to adhere to market best practices standards.
key_points: The NY Fed transacts primarily with regulated banks and broker-dealers under direction of the FOMC and Section 14 of the Federal Reserve Act
Counterparties must not pose undue credit risk exposure to the NY Fed or parties on whose behalf operations are executed
Counterparties must operate at sufficient ongoing scale to support the NY Fed's execution needs
Counterparties must meet minimum capital standards set by their primary regulator
Counterparties must adhere to TMPG Best Practices for Treasury, Agency Debt and Agency MBS Markets and FX Global Code
Counterparty types covered include: primary dealers, standing repo counterparties, reverse repo counterparties, foreign exchange counterparties, and foreign reserves management counterparties
The NY Fed may terminate any counterparty relationship at any time for any reason at its discretion
why_it_matters: This policy document establishes the foundational framework for who the Federal Reserve transacts with when executing monetary policy operations, including open market operations and repo transactions. The counterparty framework is critical market infrastructure—understanding which entities are eligible to participate in Fed operations provides insight into the plumbing of monetary policy implementation and potential concentration risks in market structure.
market_structure_context: This document describes the NY Fed's counterparty eligibility criteria and operational expectations for market operations. It covers the framework for primary dealers, repo counterparties, and FX counterparties that participate in Federal Reserve market operations. The policy explicitly references the Treasury Market Practices Group (TMPG) best practices and FX Global Code as standards counterparties must follow.
